questions.input: What process node has entered the production stage recently?
questions.answer: 7 nm

questions.input: When did Moore’s Law show signs of slowdown?
What is the main form of modular chips?
questions.answer: Chip carrier

questions.input: Which company introduced the Agilex FPGA based on chiplet technology?
questions.answer: Intel

questions.input: What is DARPA’s CHIPS plan primarily focused on?
options: Achieving strategic unification and ecological construction for chiplets
Creating chips for consumer electronics only
Developing a new type of monolithic chip
questions.answer: Achieving strategic unification and ecological construction for chiplets

questions.input: What is the primary function of an interposer in 2.5D IC packaging?
options: To act as a power source
questions.answer: To connect multiple dies electrically

questions.input: Which two materials are commonly used to make interposers?
questions.answer: Silicon and organic materials

questions.input: Compared to conventional packaging approaches, interposers are generally:
questions.answer: Less expensive

questions.input: What does TSV stand for, and what is its role in interposers?
options: Transient Signal Vector; for timing signals
Through-Silicon Via; for vertical signal transmission
Thin Silicon Variation; for size adjustment
Tuned Signal Voltage; for power regulation
questions.answer: Through-Silicon Via; for vertical signal transmission

questions.input: What does 2.5D packaging mainly involve?
questions.answer: Side-by-side dies on an interposer

questions.input: What is the main advantage of chiplet-based architectures over monolithic chip designs?
options: Reduced manufacturing cost and improved yield
questions.answer: Reduced manufacturing cost and improved yield

questions.input: What standard has the industry adopted to address die-to-die communication challenges?
questions.answer: UCIe

questions.input: Why is crosstalk a concern in chiplet systems?
options: It results from electromagnetic interference between adjacent signals
questions.answer: It results from electromagnetic interference between adjacent signals

questions.input: What does VTF measure?
questions.answer: Signal loss

questions.input: What helps ensure clean signals in UCIe?
questions.answer: Eye masks

questions.input: What is one main reason the semiconductor industry is shifting towards 3D heterogenous integration?
options: It allows chips to be manufactured entirely on a single monolithic substrate
It enables continued performance scaling beyond the limits of Mooreâ€™s Law
It eliminates the need for thermal management in high-density systems
It replaces the need for packaging altogether
It reduces the number of I/Os in complex devices
questions.answer: It enables continued performance scaling beyond the limits of Mooreâ€™s Law

questions.input: Which packaging method creates high-density connections without using full through-silicon via (TSV) stacks?
questions.answer: Fan-Out Wafer-Level Packaging (FO-WLP)

questions.input: Which of the following is an example of a passive element integrated within 3D heterogenous integration?
questions.answer: Microtransformers for galvanic insulation

questions.input: Compared to System-on-Chip (SoC), heterogenous integration is preferred when:
options: Reducing feature size is easy and cost-effective
The design requires only monolithic integration
High pitch, large I/O, and high performance are needed despite scaling limits
Only one foundry is available for all components
questions.answer: High pitch, large I/O, and high performance are needed despite scaling limits

questions.input: What is a key challenge specific to 3D stacking in heterogenous integration?
questions.answer: Overheating due to trapped heat

questions.input: Which of these is NOT typically used to improve thermal management in heterogeneous integration?
questions.answer: Additional TSV layers

questions.input: What does the BCD process in smart power ICs stand for?
questions.answer: Bipolar, CMOS, DMOS

questions.input: Which statement about Fan-Out Wafer-Level Packaging (FO-WLP) is correct?
options: It increases die size to fit more contacts
It reduces size and cost by enabling more contacts without enlarging the die
It is only suitable for ceramic substrates
It is synonymous with monolithic integration
questions.answer: It reduces size and cost by enabling more contacts without enlarging the die

questions.input: A designer wants to use a bridge structure instead of TSV interposers to connect chips laterally. Which solution best matches this goal?
questions.answer: Embedded Multi-die Interconnect Bridge (EMIB)

questions.input: In the context of functional integration for power electronics, which of the following is a benefit of evolving to 3D heterogeneous functional integration?
options: Higher voltages and power with less reliable packaging
Larger device footprint for easier cooling
Integrated control, protection, and cooling in a compact system
Reduced need for drivers and sensors
questions.answer: Integrated control, protection, and cooling in a compact system

questions.input: Which of the following technologies directly enables high-bandwidth connections in vertically stacked 3D heterogeneous integration?
questions.answer: Through-Silicon Vias (TSVs)

questions.input: In a power electronics system, which component is specifically used for short-circuit protection in smart power ICs?
questions.answer: SenseFET

questions.input: Why is chiplet technology increasingly used in modern heterogenous integration?
options: It eliminates the need for wafer-level packaging
It partitions large SoCs into smaller dies, boosting yield and cost-efficiency
It replaces the need for any form of packaging
questions.answer: It partitions large SoCs into smaller dies, boosting yield and cost-efficiency

questions.input: Which substrate type is most commonly used for heterogeneous integration in smartphones and wearables?
questions.answer: Organic substrates

questions.input: What combination best describes the BCD process commonly found in integrated low-to-medium voltage power ICs?
questions.answer: Bipolar, CMOS, DMOS

questions.input: Why has heterogeneous integration become more important in recent years?
options: It allows companies to bypass the need for AI
It replaces traditional processors with photonic-only systems
Moore’s Law scaling is slowing, limiting gains from transistor miniaturization
It removes the need for analog design in chips
It avoids the use of chiplets altogether
questions.answer: Moore’s Law scaling is slowing, limiting gains from transistor miniaturization

questions.input: What is a chiplet in the context of heterogeneous integration?
options: A type of wireless communication device
A photonic-only chip for fiber optics
A simplified user interface for chip design
A deprecated transistor model from the 1990s
A small functional block that can be integrated with others in a package
questions.answer: A small functional block that can be integrated with others in a package

questions.input: What was the primary focus of Phase I in the silicon interposer development program?
options: Designing a full system-on-chip on a single die
Fabricating a 2-metal-layer bridge interposer using a damascene process
Incorporating AI algorithms into the interposer
questions.answer: Fabricating a 2-metal-layer bridge interposer using a damascene process

questions.input: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of silicon interposers over organic interposers?
options: Compatibility of thermal expansion with silicon chips
Ability to use the damascene process to achieve fine features
questions.answer: Much lower material cost

questions.input: In phase II of the silicon interposer development program, what major structural feature is added to the interposer?
options: Embedded logic cores for real-time control
questions.answer: Through-silicon vias (TSVs)

questions.input: Which electrical test is used to measure sheet resistance?
questions.answer: Van der Pauw test

questions.input: What is the purpose of adding copper pillars and C4 bumps in test silicon interposers?
options: To test signal integrity at millimeter-wave frequencies
To see how reliable a fully assembled exemplar system would be
To enable thermal dissipation for the interposer
To allow passive RFID tracking of dies
To create a vacuum-sealed chip chamber
questions.answer: To see how reliable a fully assembled exemplar system would be

questions.input: What is the primary goal of heterogeneous integration in semiconductor manufacturing?
options: To improve performance and functionality by combining different dies in one package
To eliminate analog circuitry in chiplet design
To increase the transistor count on a single monolithic die
To reduce mask layers in lithography
To reduce the use of chiplets in high-performance computing
questions.answer: To improve performance and functionality by combining different dies in one package

questions.input: What is one benefit of placing high-performance logic and HBM close together using advanced packaging?
options: It allows for larger wire bonds
It reduces latency and power consumption
It prevents electromagnetic interference in power rails
It eliminates the need for copper
questions.answer: It reduces latency and power consumption

questions.input: Which type of heterogeneity does feature-level integration have?
questions.answer: Process-layer heterogeneity

questions.input: Which of the following is a reason why the semiconductor industry is pursuing heterogeneous integration?
options: It allows faster time to market and accelerates power and performance
Traditional scaling is still highly cost-effective
It reduces the need for logic and memory components
It simplifies binary programming at scale
questions.answer: It allows faster time to market and accelerates power and performance

questions.input: What are two major breakthroughs required for heterogeneous integration?
options: Hybrid bonding and larger advanced substrates
questions.answer: Hybrid bonding and larger advanced substrates

questions.input: Why has heterogeneous integration become more important in recent years?
options: It allows companies to bypass the need for AI
It replaces traditional processors with photonic-only systems
It eliminates the need for silicon interposers
Mooreâ€™s Law scaling is slowing, limiting gains from transistor miniaturization
It avoids the use of chiplets altogether
questions.answer: Mooreâ€™s Law scaling is slowing, limiting gains from transistor miniaturization

questions.input: Which of the following is an example of a real-world application of heterogeneous integration?
options: AMDâ€™s EPYC processors using chiplet architecture
Microsoft Excel running on ARM chips
questions.answer: AMDâ€™s EPYC processors using chiplet architecture

questions.input: What is the purpose of the Heterogenous Integration Roadmap (HIR)?
options: To guide and align industry and academic efforts in HI development
To promote the exclusive use of silicon photonics
To delay the adoption of chiplet architectures
To consolidate all foundries into a single provider
To stop development of chiplets in favor of monolithic designs
questions.answer: To guide and align industry and academic efforts in HI development

questions.input: Which of the following is a benefit or trend associated with heterogeneous integration?
options: Eliminating the need for any form of thermal management in high-performance systems
Allowing chips to function independently without communication interfaces
Reducing the cost of DRAM by using monolithic memory integration exclusively
Reducing the system size and the number of components and peripherals needed
Phasing out chiplets in favor of single-die SoC designs for all applications
questions.answer: Reducing the system size and the number of components and peripherals needed

questions.input: What is heterogenous integration in the context of integrated chips?
options: The integration of identical components on a chip
Incorporating different types of semiconductor components into one chip or package
Using only silicon-based technology for all components
Replacing memory with sensors in chip design
Combining only memory and I/O units
questions.answer: Incorporating different types of semiconductor components into one chip or package

questions.input: What key challenge does heterogeneous integration aim to address?
options: The rising cost of internet services
Difficulty scaling transistors in traditional 2D ICs
Transitioning from silicon to plastic chips
questions.answer: Difficulty scaling transistors in traditional 2D ICs

questions.input: Which of the following is a major benefit of heterogeneous integration?
options: It eliminates the need for silicon
It allows uniformity in all components
It enables performance optimization by using the best technology for each function
questions.answer: It enables performance optimization by using the best technology for each function

questions.input: Why is Moore's Law slowing down?
options: Lack of demand for smaller devices
Increased cost and complexity of shrinking gate lengths
questions.answer: Increased cost and complexity of shrinking gate lengths

questions.input: What is one example of heterogeneous integration in everyday technology?
options: Smart thermostats combining sensors, processors, and AI
questions.answer: Smart thermostats combining sensors, processors, and AI

questions.input: Which of the following is not a type of heterogeneous integration?
questions.answer: Uniform IC replication

questions.input: What defines a System-on-Chip (SoC)?
options: A chip with only memory and I/O
A single chip integrating most or all system functions
A chip that uses no silicon
A chip dedicated only to sensors
questions.answer: A single chip integrating most or all system functions

questions.input: What distinguishes System-in-Package (SiP) from SoC?
options: SiP has a higher integration level than SoC
SiP integrates functions at the package level, not the chip level
SoC uses more wires than SiP
SoC does not use modern packaging
questions.answer: SiP integrates functions at the package level, not the chip level

questions.input: What is the role of an interposer in advanced packaging?
options: Acting as a bridge between chips to provide high-speed data transmission
questions.answer: Acting as a bridge between chips to provide high-speed data transmission

questions.input: Through-Silicon Vias (TSVs) are used primarily for what purpose?
options: Creating vertical electrical connections between chip layers
questions.answer: Creating vertical electrical connections between chip layers

questions.input: What is a benefit of Fan-Out Wafer Level Packaging (FOWLP)?
options: Offers flexibility and reduced form factor
questions.answer: Offers flexibility and reduced form factor

questions.input: Which materials are often integrated with silicon for high-frequency and optical applications?
questions.answer: Gallium arsenide (GaAs) and indium phosphide (InP)

questions.input: What does MEMS stand for?
questions.answer: Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems

questions.input: What is a common advantage of SoC over SiP?
options: Lower power consumption and smaller form factor
questions.answer: Lower power consumption and smaller form factor

questions.input: Which applications are driving the demand for heterogeneous integration in the future?
options: Artificial intelligence, HPC, and data centers
questions.answer: Artificial intelligence, HPC, and data centers
